title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Today we are going to talk about my experiences celebrating Chuseok in Korea as an American.
Oh, I heard that you like this kind of chocolate, so I'm going to buy you this kind of chocolate.

title: will / shall
examples: Our ancestors will understand.
Yeah, we'll see your family and have dinner.
You'll walk in and the living room floor is covered in gifts.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: It is kind of fun when you have a bunch of things to open.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Maybe we can have a dinner one day or something like that.
One two-stop food that I can never understand though is spam.
You can barely walk.

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: I was like, oh, there must be an extra zero here.

title: have to / must
examples: My mom has to work for like a couple of days before.
You had to book way in advance and everybody's ready to go at a certain time to try to reserve a seat.

title: should (advice)
examples: Like father, like son, I'm American, you're Korean, so we should celebrate both cultures.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: I would never think of giving someone spam as a gift in the US.
I think they would be really weirded out and maybe offended.
What would you guys like more?

title: If I do / If I did
examples: If we have kids, what holidays are we going to celebrate?

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: They were excited to see you, actually.
I think it's a little bit of a waste of money and time to be honest for the tradition.
You'll walk in and the living room floor is covered in gifts.
I want to teach what Jusok is about to them.

title: Passive (special: get + p.p.)
examples: And then I remember my mom sometimes getting tired or even getting sick after the event.
